records.ety: [OE. fend, find, fiend, feond, fiend, foe, AS. fend; akin to OS. fīond, D. vijand enemy, OHG. fīant, G. feind, Icel. fjānd, Sw. & Dan. fiende, Goth. fijands; orig. p. pr. of a verb meaning to hate, AS. fen, fegan, OHG. fī<?/n, Goth. fijan, Skr. pīy to scorn; prob. akin to E. feud a quarrel. √81. Cf. Foe, Friend.]

defs: An implacable or malicious foe; one who is diabolically wicked or cruel; an infernal being; -- applied specifically to the devil or a demon.
quotes: Into this wild abyss the wary fiend Stood on the brink of Hell and looked a while.
O woman! woman! when to ill thy mind Is bent, all hell contains no fouler fiend.
